Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Achieving superior elevated temperature properties in additive manufactured nickel-based superalloys through unique alloy design

Composition_Info.Note: Balance is Ni. Measured composition from powder EDS/ICP analysis.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: LPBF fabricated on Concept Laser M2 machine with laser power 300 W, scanning speed 1100 mm/s, layer thickness 50 um, hatch spacing 100 um. Strip scanning strategy employed, new layer rotated 67 deg relative to previously deposited layer. Argon atmosphere. Post-printed HIP heat treatment at 1175 C for 4 h under 160 MPa isostatic pressure, followed by argon cooled to room temperature.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF (300 W, 1100 mm/s, 50 um layer, 100 um hatch, 67 deg rotation, Ar). HIP treatment (1175 C, 4 h, 160 MPa, Ar cooled).

Microstructure_Text.original: As-printed H230 sample exhibits severe cracking with crack density of 2.81 mm/mm2. After HIP heat treatment at 1175C for 4h under 160 MPa, cracks are completely annihilated. Granular precipitated phases (W-rich M6C carbides) with average size of 1.3 +- 0.2 um at grain boundaries and 0.45 +- 0.05 um within grains are distributed. Precipitate-free zone occurs on both sides of the grain boundary due to coarsening of intergranular M6C consuming carbon in surrounding area. Intragranular M6C precipitated along cell boundaries. Crack density after HIP: 0. Grain size: 13.2 um after heat treatment.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Severe as-printed cracking (2.81 mm/mm2) eliminated by HIP. W-rich M6C carbides at GB (1.3 um) and intragranular (0.45 um). Precipitate-free zones near GB. FCC matrix.

Composition_Info.Note: Balance is Ni. 1 wt% Zr added to Haynes 230 for crack-free AM. Measured composition from powder EDS/ICP analysis.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Laser Powder Bed Fusion (LPBF) + Heat Treatment

Process_Text.original: LPBF fabricated on Concept Laser M2 machine with laser power 300 W, scanning speed 1100 mm/s, layer thickness 50 um, hatch spacing 100 um. Strip scanning strategy employed, new layer rotated 67 deg relative to previously deposited layer. Argon atmosphere. Heat treatment in muffle furnace at 1200 C for 1 h, then air cooled to room temperature.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF (300 W, 1100 mm/s, 50 um layer, 100 um hatch, 67 deg rotation, Ar). Heat treatment (1200 C, 1 h, air cooled).

Microstructure_Text.original: As-printed H230AM shows complete crack elimination with significantly higher fraction of segregation phases at cell boundaries compared to H230. Network segregation phases (Ni-Zr phase) completely dissolved after atmospheric heat treatment at 1200 C for 1h. Extensive ZrC carbide precipitates with size 166 +- 18 nm uniformly distributed in matrix along cell and grain boundaries. Carbide number density: 1.71 um-2 (vs 0.52 um-2 for H230). Coarse carbides along grain boundaries suppressed. Cell boundaries preserved after heat treatment enabling uniform ZrC distribution. After creep at 900C/65 MPa: ZrC carbides maintained, M6C carbides (~380 nm) at grain boundaries, plate-like Ni7Zr2 phase (~300 nm, orientation (001)Ni7Zr2 // (111)gamma) within grains. Matrix distortion stripes observed in matrix with orientation relationship to (022) plane.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Crack-free LPBF microstructure. Uniform fine ZrC carbides (166 nm) at cell/GB. Ni7Zr2 phase along (111) close-packed planes after creep. Matrix distortion stripes. FCC matrix.
